title: Marketing Admin / Assistant
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a highly organized, energetic, and detail-oriented Marketing Admin/Assistant to join our growing marketing team. This role is the operational backbone of the marketing department — keeping the team on track, managing communications, organizing assets, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.



This is an excellent opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys process and structure, and takes pride in keeping a team running smoothly. While this is an administrative and coordination-focused role, the right candidate will have direct visibility into all marketing functions and will play a meaningful part in our team's daily success.



Key Attributes



Strong Communicator

Highly Organized

High Energy & Driven

Proactive & Detail-Oriented





The ideal candidate is self-sufficient and comfortable working independently across time zones. They communicate proactively, follow through consistently, and hold themselves accountable without needing to be micromanaged.



Responsibilities



Admin & Operations

*   Maintain the Marketing Meeting Agenda document for bi-weekly Tuesday tea
etings

             - Follow up with tea
mbers before each meeting to gather inputs on campaigns, performance, and blockers.                

               Build and organize the draft agenda with clear sections: Key Takeaways, Action Items, and Updates

*   Monitor the shared marketing inbox daily; flag urgent messages and route to the appropriate tea
mber

*   Send follow-up messages to the team after meetings; track responses on assigned tasks and escalate anything missing or delayed

*   Update and maintain task boards (MOS); mark task progress, follow up with task owners for status updates, and keep everything current

*   Follow up on and manage internal operational requests to ensure timely resolution

*   Manage and order business cards for tea
mbers as needed

*   Respond to all Google Business reviews in a timely and professional manner using preset, pre-approved response templates

*  Manage ad-hoc tasks assigned



Content & Asset Management

*   Organize and maintain Google Drive folders; uphold naming conventions and ensure videos, graphics, and reports are easy to locate

*   Organize 
 project folders with clear labeling and folder structure

*   Manage YouTube publishing workflow: switch videos from unlisted to public after the required 24–48 hour hold period

*   Maintain the content calendar: input posts, update statuses (Idea ? In Progress ? Ready for Approval ? Scheduled ? Posted), and keep the calendar accurate

*   Collect content from designers and editors; organize for review; route for approval; track Approved vs. Needs Revision status; communicate updates back to the team

*   Respond to social media direct messages using preset, pre-approved response templates

*   Update the PR sheet immediately upon receipt of any publication request or press notice — accuracy and timeliness are critical for this task

*   Manage the end-to-end podcast guest process: log guest information in Salesforce and handle all guest communications from initial outreach through confirmation to arranging intro call



Book — Order Management & Tracking

*   Manage customer-facing order error communications: reach out to customers experiencing order issues and provide resolution or escalation

*   Track all book orders and maintain up-to-date records on order status



Qualifications & Requirements

*   Minimum 5 years of experience in the related field

*   Associate's or Bachelor's degree preferred

*   Excellent written and verbal English communication skills — clear, professional, and responsive

*   Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks, deadlines, and workflows simultaneously

*   High energy, self-motivated, and driven — able to work independently with minimal supervision

*   Proficiency with standard productivity tools: Google Workspace (Drive, Docs, Sheets, Gmail, Calendar)

*   Familiarity with project management or task board tools 

*   Comfortable working with social media platforms for monitoring and response purposes

*   Experience with CRM tools such as Salesforce is a plus

*   Experience with content platforms such as 
 and YouTube Studio is a plus

*   Prior experience in a marketing support or operations coordinator role preferred

*   Reliable internet connection and a dedicated, distraction-free remote work setup

*   Available to work during US business hours



Qualifications & Requirements

Fully remote, long-term opportunity with a growing marketing team

Stable hours and consistent workload

Direct exposure to marketing operations, content workflows, and client communications

Collaborative, fast-paced team environment that values initiative and follow-through





Interested candidates should submit a resume, an intro video, and a brief cover note describing their experience managing administrative and coordination tasks in a remote environment.



About Falcon Wealth Planning

Falcon Wealth Planning is a fiduciary, fee-only wealth advisory firm on a mission to redefine how Americans experience financial planning. We translate complex tax and investment strategies into clear, human stories that build trust, because wealth decisions are emotional before they’re logical.

title: Direct Response Copywriter (Short-Form Video Ads – Meta)
description:
I’m hiring a direct response copywriter for short-form video ads on
 and Instagram.



- This is NOT a UGC role (no acting)

- This is NOT a static ad role (no graphic design)

- This is NOT e-mail marketing

- This is not a social media manager role



This is a results-driven ad writing role focused on Meta 
/Instagram) conversions.

___

Your job is simple: write hooks that make cat owners:

- Stop scrolling

- Feel something

- Rethink how they’re treating their cat… 

- Then connect into a proven ad body already created for you.



You’ll be writing short-form VSL-style scripts (30–90 seconds), but you only need to create the hooks and angles. The main body is already done for you.

___

I’m looking for someone who can write lines like:

“You’re a
 hurting your cat everyday, and you don’t even realize it.”

“I’ve had 4 cats in my life… and I’m embarrassed to say I just learned this.”

"There’s a 
nt every cat parent eventually faces…"



Not:

“Does your cat scratch the couch?”

“Is your cat bored?”

"Is your cat lonely?"



ChatGPT can do this already.

___

The MOST important part of writing a script is creating a good first line (pattern interrupter) to make people stop scrolling,

then introducing an angle/problem in a storytelling fashion, to get people interested to keep listening. 

We want people to feel like they are watching organic content. 

***If you can't get them to stop scrolling, the rest of the script is useless.

___



You need to understand:

- Scroll-stopping pattern interrupters

- Fast, natural, storytelling flow (no boring parts)

- Selling without sounding like an ad

- Writing for video (short lines, voiceover pacing, B-roll flow)

___



Daily work is simple:

- Think of a ad set angle

- write 3 hook variations per day

- About 1-2 hours per day, depending on your speed

- Monday–Friday.

___



Pay is 10,000 PHP/month for a 30-day trial for part time work. If you create a profitable ad, this can increase to 20k–30k PHP/month, or SUBSTANTIALLY more depending on output. 

***Bottom line, if you can create ads that generate profit, you can make your price. 

___



Hiring Process:

1) Send me a message here, showing that you understand how this job works.

***I'm going to get a lot of applications, so if you can't get me interested in your application right away, you won't be able to stop people from scrolling. 



2) If we feel you have potential, I’ll send you a few top-performing ad examples and you’ll write 3 hooks using the same structure. I’ll send 300 PHP upfront before you start for your practice ad. (I only pay on 
) 



3) If you do that well, I'll send you 2,500php  for 5 days of work, for 1 ad set per day (one angle with 3 hook variations)



4) If you submit good work, have good communication, and good work ethic, we will do 3 more weeks.



5) If you do well for a month, we can discuss increasing salary and output. 



6) After 3 months, you will start earning commission based on ad performance. 



***As I said earlier, if you know how to make profitable ads right away, probationary period is meaningless, and we can increase your salary instantly. 



Good luck!

title: Data Enrichment / Web Scraping Specialist
description:
Data Enrichment & Web Scraping Specialist

JT Media is a marketing agency that helps healthcare companies recruit new job candidates. We are in need of a full-time (8hr/day, 5 days a week) Data Enrichment & Web Scraping Specialist to build, manage, and scale our backend data systems.

Your primary role will be as follows:

Data Sourcing & Web Scraping. You will identify and pull data from online resources including government databases, directories, and other public sources. This includes scraping large datasets efficiently using tools or systems (not manual-only work). Much of this data will only have company name, address, and phone number. Your job will be to find the desirable contacts at these companies based on the project. At a minimum, we are looking for a verified work
.

Data Enrichment. You will take raw data and enrich it with accurate contact information 
s, phone numbers, LinkedIn profiles, etc.) using both tools and manual methods when needed. We are looking for someone who has knowledge on tools that can effectively do this at scale.

s.

title: Funding Coordinator Assistant (Remote – Alternative Lending
description:
About Us



We’re a fast-growing alternative lending company helping entrepreneurs and business owners secure the capital they need — from personal term loans and business credit cards to SBA products, equipment funding, and business credit lines.



Our team moves fast. We help clients through every stage of the funding process — from pre-qualification to approval — and pride ourselves on providing white-glove service that makes financing feel simple.



The Challenge



The hard part in lending isn’t selling the money — it’s fulfilling it.

Our Funding Coordinator is managing a heavy pipeline — chasing documents, packaging applications, submitting to lenders, and tracking approvals.



That’s where you come in.



The Role: Funding Coordinator Assistant



You’ll be the right hand to our senior Funding Coordinator — helping keep deals organized, documents collected, and clients informed.

Your job is to make sure every file moves smoothly from “application” to “funded.”



Note: You may do all or part of these responsibilities depending on workflow, volume, and your skill set.



Your daily responsibilities:



Follow up with clients for missing documents (bank statements, tax returns, IDs, etc.)



Upload and organize documents in Google Drive and our CRM (GoHighLevel)



Maintain trackers and spreadsheets with deal status, lender, and progress notes



Communicate with clients via
, text, and Slack for updates and reminders



Prepare lender submission packets under guidance from the Funding Coordinator



Create daily progress reports on active files and pending docs



Assist in credit optimization — helping clients prepare for funding by:



Reviewing personal and business credit profiles



Tracking action plans to improve credit scores and utilization



Keeping clients accountable to paydowns, tradeline additions, and monitoring goals



Ensure clients always feel informed, supported, and confident throughout the process



Who You Are



Extremely organized — you love checklists and keeping multiple projects on track



Strong communicator — professional, clear, and concise in English



Detail-oriented — accuracy matters to you



Tech-savvy — comfortable with CRMs (GoHighLevel), Google Drive, and Slack



Reliable and proactive — you take initiative and follow through



Experience in lending, banking, credit repair, or financial services is a strong plus



What’s In It for You



Fully remote role with a growing U.S.-based company



Pay: $4–$6/hr USD (depending on experience)



Performance bonuses tied to deals funded



Work directly with experienced funding professionals



Learn the inside playbook of credit and business lending



Clear growth path to Client Success Manager or Funding Coordinator as we scale



How to Apply



Send your:



Resume



Short video or Loom (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you’d be a great fit



Your internet speed test and available working hours (in EST)

title: Advanced Dispute Specialist (SALARY +BONUSES)
description:
Who We Are

Altura Funding is a business funding and capital solutions company based in Cleveland, Ohio. We move fast and we expect results. We are bringing our credit repair operations fully in-house and we need someone who already knows what they are doing — not someone we have to train from scratch.



What This Role Is

You will manage consumer credit dispute campaigns on behalf of our clients using legally compliant, aggressive dispute strategies under the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA). You will challenge inaccurate, unverifiable, and outdated negative items across all three credit bureaus. This is not a basic dispute role. We expect sophisticated, consumer-law-based strategies executed at high volume with fast turnaround.



What You Will Do



Draft and submit customized, consumer-law-based dispute letters to Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion



Challenge negative items using FCRA Section 611 (accuracy and completeness), Section 605 (obsolete items), and Section 623 (furnisher responsibilities)



Use FDCPA-based debt validation letters to challenge collection accounts



Write goodwill adjustment requests, pay-for-delete proposals, and method of verification demands



Track all dispute timelines, bureau responses, and client file updates with meticulous accuracy



Coordinate with our team on client readiness for business funding as credit profiles improve



Manage multiple client files simultaneously without missing deadlines



Communicate client progress clearly and on schedule



Who You Are



You are not someone who logs into Credit Repair Cloud, DisputeBee, or any other platform and blasts out generic templates. We have seen what that produces and it is not good enough for our clients. We need someone who understands the law, knows how to craft custom dispute arguments, and can move the needle fast.



You know that real results come from understanding why a negative item is on the report, identifying the correct legal angle to challenge it, and executing with precision. You do not guess. You do not wait. You know that bureaus have 30 days to respond and you use every tool available within that window to get results.



If you need six months to start producing, this is not the role for you. Our clients need movement quickly and we expect to see results within the first 30 days of working a file. If you have a proven track record and documented results to show for it, we want to hear from you. If you do not, please do not apply.



What You Must Have



Minimum 2 years of hands-on credit dispute experience — not just using software, actually writing and managing disputes from strategy to resolution



Deep working knowledge of FCRA, FDCPA, and Metro 2 compliance standards



Ability to draft original, legally grounded dispute letters — not template blasts



Experience working directly with all three major bureaus and their dispute processes



Familiarity with credit repair platforms but the ability to operate beyond their built-in templates



Extreme attention to detail and the ability to manage high volume without errors



Strong written English communication skills



Documented results you can show during the interview — we will ask



What We Do Not Do

We operate strictly within the bounds of federal consumer protection law. We do not promise specific score increases, we do not dispute accurate information, and we do not use deceptive or fraudulent practices. If that is not how you operate, this is not the right fit.



Compensation

Competitive base pay based on experience. Performance bonuses tied to documented client results. Long-term opportunity for the right person.



To Apply

Send your resume, a description of your credit repair experience, and at least two documented examples of dispute outcomes you have achieved for clients. Applications without proven results will not be reviewed.

title: Product Designer – Longshot Labs
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a Product Designer to be the first dedicated design hire at Longshot, a prediction market focused on deep liquidity and user experience. You will work directly with the founders to build the product experience from the ground up, owning everything from initial ideation to production-ready specs.

What You'll Do

Own the core product experience 

You'll be the design voice for Longshot's most critical surfaces, including the trading and markets pages where users place predictions, and the onboarding flows that turn new visitors into active traders. You'll think in real interactions: order states, loading behaviors, edge cases, empty states, and the small 
nts that make a product feel trustworthy rather than thrown together.

Build and evolve the design system 

You will take ownership of a design system that has some initial components, but is still in development. You will walk through what already exists, establish component standards, define tokens, and build a library that scales as the product grows. Your system will be the foundation that lets the rest of the team ship faster without losing consistency.

Drive ideation to handoff 

You will be in the room from the earliest conversations through to engineering handoff. That means contributing to requirements discussions, iterating quickly on concepts, producing clean Figma files with clear components and annotations, and providing clarifications as engineers perform wiring.

Think like an owner 

You'll have bi-weekly design reviews directly with the founders and a seat at the table on product decisions. We want someone who voices their opinions when something doesn't serve the user and measures success by the look of the final product.

Drive decisions related to graphic, branding, and marketing assets

Longshot's visual identity doesn't stop at the app. You'll own how we look everywhere else too: the marketing site, brand assets, graphics, all of it. Same care, same eye, same standards as the product. When something looks off, you'll catch it. And you'll fix it.

What We're Looking For

You have 1 to 4 years of experience as a product or UX designer, ideally at a startup. You're deeply comfortable in Figma. You use AI tools to naturally complement your workflow, but major decisions are still driven by your thinking. You have strong interaction design instincts and think in systems and flows, not just individual screens. You are intrinsically motivated and thrive in a remote environment.

Bonus points if you have

Prior exposure to crypto, consumer-facing applications, and/or gaming interfaces

Experience building or contributing to a design system early on

Some background in user research or usability testing

An eye for motion and microinteractions that make dense UIs feel alive

What success looks like

In your first few months, you'll have continued to maintain the design system, owned end-to-end design for at least two major product surfaces, and built a design rhythm with the founding team that keeps quality high as we move quickly. Over time, you'll become the design standard-bearer for Longshot, and as the team grows, you'll help define what great design looks like.

What We Offer

Meaningful ownership with significant equity as the first design hire

Direct access to founders with a real voice in product direction from day one

A fully remote, high-trust environment where you own your work

Real impact: your designs ship to real users quickly



 me at 
 with your portfolio if interested

title: Sales & Tech Representative
description:
We’re Hiring!!!



Are you a skilled salesperson with a background in gas plumbing and/or electrical work? Do you enjoy helping customers find the right solution while using your technical knowledge? Join our growing team and work with a US online home goods retailer that values expertise, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.





Position:

Sales & Tech Representative – Technical Products (Gas & Electrical Focus)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST (40 hours/week)

Salary: Competitive + Performance Bonuses





About the Role:

As a Salesperson, you will assist customers with product selection, provide technical support during the sales process, and close sales with confidence. You’ll work with gas fireplaces, heaters, stoves, and other appliances, so previous hands-on experience or technical knowledge of gas or electrical systems is a MUST.





Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Help customers choose the right gas or electric heating appliance for their needs

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

Explain technical specifications and installation requirements in simple terms

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them





Requirements:

Sales Experience – 2+ years, preferably with B2C or B2B technical products

Gas Plumbing or Electrical Experience – Formal training or work experience (installer, technician, or similar roles preferred)

Excellent English – Both written and spoken

Confident Communicator – Can explain complex concepts to non-technical customers

Tech-Savvy – Comfortable using CRMs,
 platforms, order tools, etc.

Reliable and Proactive – Takes initiative and works independently

Home Office Setup – Stable internet, noise-free environment, backup power is a plus





Preferred but Not Required:

Knowledge of venting, HVAC, or hearth appliances

US product or code familiarity

Experience in the construction or home improvement industry





Why Join Us?

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping cost. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position





How to Apply:

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph with the subject line: "Sales Pro - [Your Name]"

title: Interior Designer (Full-Time, Remote) – Build Magical Spaces for a Global Hospitality Brand
description:
We’re looking for a full-time Interior Designer to join a fast-growing hospitality brand that builds immersive, story-driven spaces, often described as the Disney of short stays.



This is not a freelance or side-hustle role.



This is a full-time, exclusive position for a designer who wants to fully commit to one brand, own projects end-to-end, and build a long-term creative career.



If you’re looking to juggle multiple clients or keep other professional commitments, this role will not be the right fit.





About the Role:



You will be the sole Interior Designer responsible for running design projects from start to finish.



That means you will:



- Lead each project from concept to final delivery

- Drive timelines, milestones, and 
ntum

- Run kick-offs, reviews, and design sessions

- Own modelling, layouts, FF&E, and specifications

- Proactively push projects forward without being chased

- Collaborate with a creative director for vision and alignment



You won’t be given tasks.

You’ll be given ownership.





Exclusivity & Focus (Important):



This role requires full professional focus.





We’re looking for someone who:



- Treats this as their only professional design role

- Does not take on other client work or employment

- Wants to invest their creative energy into building one brand deeply

- Thinks and operates as if this were their own business



We believe the quality and pace of work we’re aiming for is only possible with complete focus and commitment.





The Kind of Designer We’re Looking For



This role is for someone who:

- Thinks like an owner, not an employee

- Is highly proactive and self-driven

- Is comfortable being the single point of responsibility

- Is responsive and reliable in fast-moving environments

- Takes pride in their work and their craft

- Wants to build a long-term career, not just complete jobs



We care far more about ownership, judgement, and 
ntum than about where you studied or how many years you’ve worked.



How We Work:



- Fully remote

- Outcome-driven

- High autonomy, high trust

- Fast-moving projects

- Real creative freedom



The only strict expectation is responsiveness when tagged or messaged, because that’s how projects keep moving.





Compensation Structure



- Monthly base pay

- Plus a performance-based bonus per project onboarded



High performers are directly rewarded for the projects they successfully deliver.



Exact figures will be discussed with shortlisted candidates.





What Success Looks Like



In your first 90 days, you’ll be:



- Running multiple projects independently

- Driving timelines without being prompted

- Producing high-quality, immersive designs

- Becoming the creative owner of our spaces





How to Apply



Please include:



- A link to your portfolio

- A short video answering these questions, uploaded to a public google drive link:



“This role requires full professional focus with no other work commitments. Why does that suit you at this stage of your career?” and "If someone was to tell you, you were going to join the Disney of the short-stay industry, how would that make you feel?"





If you’re looking for a place to truly own your craft and grow long-term, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Floor Plan Editor
description:
Full-time | 40 hours per week | 7:30 AM–4:00 PM ACDT | Adelaide, South Australia



Clique Creative is South Australia’s go-to for top-tier photography, videography, and marketing services. Our vision is to craft and inspire high-quality, consistent marketing solutions for property, people, and projects—while delivering a premium customer experience and fostering a fun, supportive workplace.



We’re looking for a highly skilled and detail-driven Floor Plan Editor to join our vibrant team. This position is a work-from-home position, working 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday, 7:00AM to 3:00PM ACDT, in alignment with our production and scheduling needs. 



As the Floor Plan Editor, you will play a central role in keeping our production pipeline running smoothly each day. A key component of your work involves quality controlling floor plans each day for accuracy, completing edits in Adobe Illustrator, forecasting workloads, and ensuring all outputs meet client and agency standards.  This is a very important part of our business and floor plan accuracy is paramount.



Key Responsibilities - Floor Plan Quality Control:

- Conduct daily checks on floor plans for accuracy, consistency, and compliance with standards.

- Complete edits in Adobe Illustrator software.

- Forecast upcoming floor plan workloads and assess resource needs.

- Identify, record, and communicate discrepancies or design issues.

- Maintain workflow efficiency to meet strict daily deadlines.



About You

You will succeed in this role if you:



- Have excellent English, written and verbal

- Have exceptional attention to detail.

- Have 3 years minimum experience in a similar position.

- Are confident using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.

- Are comfortable with Google Drive, Gmail, and Mac/iOS systems.

- Are highly organised and efficient with task prioritisation.

- Thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

- Enjoy interacting with clients and colleagues.

- Work well independently and as part of a supportive team.

Why Join Clique Creative?



- Join a fun, supportive, and passionate creative team.

- Work in an environment that values quality, innovation, consistency, and teamwork.

- Enjoy a varied role with opportunities to grow your operational and creative skills.

- Be part of a well-regarded creative services company supporting property, people, and projects across South Australia.



How to Apply



If you’re ready to join a dynamic, quality-driven creative team, we’d love to hear from you.

 outlining why you’re the right fit for this role.

title: GoHighLevel EXPERT
description:
We are a marketing agency.

We need a GoHighLevel expert to build and manage the full backend system for our clinic clients. This is a long-term full-time position with room to grow as we scale.



*What you will be building and managing:



CRM setup with custom pipelines and contact fields for each client

SMS and
 drip sequences (speed-to-lead, nurture, appointment reminders, no-show recovery, re-engagement)

Automated workflows (lead comes in from
 ad ? instant SMS fires ?
 fires ? lead enters pipeline ? notifications sent)

Calendar and booking system setup with Google Calendar sync

Missed call text-back configuration

Reputation management workflows (automated Google review requests after appointments)

Reporting dashboards for clients

Deploying GHL snapshots to new client sub-accounts and customizing for each clinic

Troubleshooting broken workflows and maintaining system health



Requirements:



Minimum 1 year using GoHighLevel daily (not just watching tutorials — actually building workflows and automations for clients)

Experience building SMS and
 sequences

Comfortable with Zapier or Make for occasional external integrations

Reliable internet and available during US business hours (EST preferred, some overlap required)

Strong written English for writing SMS and
 templates



EXTRA:

Experience with Meta Ads and CAPI Integrations - Tracking Meta Ad Performance with GHL



This is a full-time role. You will start by building our master client template (snapshot) and then deploy and manage it for each new clinic we onboard. As we grow you will be our go-to person for everything inside GoHighLevel.

title: Automotive Operations and Dispatch Coordinator
description:
Key Responsibilities



1. Fleet & Retail Ops Coordination

*Own the estimate-to-approval-to-route workflow:

*Create and submit estimates

*Follow up for approval

*Ensure parts are sourced

*Finalize tech routes for the following day

*Track all jobs in progress and flag issues proactively

*Ensure routes are realistic and efficient; confirm techs are prepped and ready

*Communicate clearly with customers and fleet managers about job timing, status, and follow-ups

*Submit and close out fleet approvals in Element, LeasePlan, and AutoIntegrate

*Ensure invoices match approvals; follow up on outstanding invoices and missing payments



2. Scheduling & Productivity Tracking

*Maintain the daily technician schedule, making updates as needed

*Monitor tech productivity; flag idle time, reschedules, or delays

*Track time logs and coordinate with bookkeeper for payroll



3. Customer Communication

*Respond to incoming texts, WhatsApp messages, and system inquiries in a timely, professional manner

*Follow up with clients as needed to resolve open issues or gather job-related info

*Escalate concerns appropriately and promptly



4. Admin & Reporting

*Submit a detailed Daily Recap covering completed work, pending tasks, blockers, and KPIs

*Keep Trello and other tools up-to-date with accurate job and task statuses

*Track inventory needs and coordinate ordering of parts, oil, and supplies

*Monitor A/R and follow up on past-due fleet invoices



5. Marketing & Content Support

*Publish our monthly newsletter in MailChimp with content from marketing team

*Coordinate publishing of content to Instagram /
 / Google My Business

*Other light admin support as needed for the marketing team



What Success Looks Like

*All work orders, estimates, approvals, and tech routes are handled with zero dropped balls

*You identify blockers or problems before they become fires

*Communication is clear, timely, and professional — with both customers and internal team

*The business runs like a machine — nothing gets missed or delayed

*You take full ownership of your responsibilities and require very little follow-up



Who You Are

*A natural systems thinker who thrives on clarity, organization, and ownership

*Extremely proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to do

*Excellent at written and verbal English communication

*Comfortable in the automotive world and eager to learn the details

*Sharp and reliable — you retain info, spot gaps, and follow through



Experience & Skills Required

*3+ years of experience in a remote automotive: coordinator, dispatcher, operations role

*Demonstrated responsibility for keeping things moving without handholding

*Excellent written and spoken English

*Experience with dispatch, route planning, or technician scheduling

*Familiar with tools like Slack, Google Docs, Trello (or similar), CRM/invoicing platforms

*Experience working with U.S.-based field service, auto, or fleet companies

*Bonus: Familiarity with AutoIntegrate, Housecall Pro, Element, or LeasePlan platforms



Required Software Experience

*Slack

*WhatsApp

*Trello (or similar)

*Google Workspace

*CRM / invoicing tools (Housecall Pro, AutoIntegrate)

* Monday . com (plus)



TECH REQUIREMENTS:

*Computer - at least i5 or equivalent with 8gb RAM

*Internet Speed - At least 50 MBPS Download speed

*Dual monitor setup is required to ensure efficiency in managing multiple applications simultaneously.

*Location: Remote (Philippines)

*Schedule: Part-time or Full-time, US hours



*Part-time Schedule

**Tuesday 1-5 pm

**Wednesday 1-5 pm

**Friday 8:30 - 7:30 pm

**Saturday 10-4 pm

**Sunday 2-5 pm



*Full-time Schedule

**Monday to Friday 9:30AM -6:30PM

Pay: Php288.46 - Php432.69 hourly rate



Application Instructions:

Fill out this form: 

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.
